items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again...
Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Preserves the mock-heroic phrasing (“vague doubts haunt me”) and the clipped parallelism that makes the bureaucrat’s suspicion sound theatrically grand. Also correctly avoids turning it into simple possession (“has”), keeping the sense of linked losses/objects as in the reference analysis.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Keeps the era-clash humor: banal modern politeness and repetitive self-introduction (“the Tsar”) instead of archaic royal address, matching Bunsha’s awkward, drunken performative politeness.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Preserves the sudden authoritarian, pompous demand that is funny precisely because it’s overblown and status-hungry coming from Bunsha.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Maintains the archaic/high-register melancholy and the comic mismatch with the mundane Soviet setting; “old woman” keeps the Tsar’s dismissive, anachronistic address.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Correctly makes “demons” the agent and keeps the repetitive panic rhythm that sells the misunderstanding of technology as sorcery.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Preserves the elevated, religiously charged proclamation that creates the joke in an atheist-modern context.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Captures the emcee-like command that triggers mass dancing; keeps the performative, public-announcement feel rather than a flat literal “Everyone dance!”

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Keeps the breezy self-congratulation and the comic understatement of a burglar delighted by his “lucky” timing.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Crucially avoids calling the eggplant spread “caviar,” preserving the Soviet-satire logic and preventing cultural confusion, while keeping the punchline timing with the pause/ellipsis.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Preserves the comic inflation-by-enumeration and the self-correcting, performative listing with ellipses that signals Shpak’s status-obsessed exaggeration.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the approved reference. Keeps the calm, sing-song resignation and the shared-absurdity punch, with ellipses preserving the rhythm and comedic escalation.

summary.interpretation: 0.00 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.00 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
